label: PI-004 cohort — `requirement` methodology entity type end-to-end (migration, access, REST, UI, tests). Second of the four PI-004 sibling builds (others: field shipped under SES-083, manual_config and test_spec remain). Migration 0015 adds the requirements table with the MoSCoW priority enum and extends refs/change_log CHECKs to admit `requirement` and all five new `requirement_*` relationship kinds proactively. Access/repositories/requirement.py + Requirement model + REQUIREMENT_STATUSES/PRIORITIES/STATUS_TRANSITIONS vocab + five new REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS kinds + four active `_kinds_for_pair` clauses (requirement → domain/entity/field/process; the fifth, (requirement, test_spec), is held as a TODO comment awaiting that PI-004 sibling). Eight REST endpoints (no atomicity deviation — references are decomposed); eight ui/client.py methods; Requirements sidebar entry at Methodology position #4 (between Processes and CRM Candidates per the build prompt spec); RequirementsPanel with the five-column master pane (Identifier/Name/Priority/Status/Updated per AC-11) + acceptance-summary editor + title-cased Priority combo; CRUD dialogs; 70 new tests. WT-051 was created in SES-081 but not transitioned by this apply (pre-existing pattern shared with SES-082/SES-083; consumes manually post-apply if desired). PI-004 is ADDRESSED (not resolved) — two siblings remain (manual_config, test_spec); the last sibling's build-closure session resolves PI-004 per DEC-232 / SES-074 pattern. The exporter gained the `requirements` entry (Requirement model in _EXPORT_TABLES); field is still missing from the exporter (pre-existing gap from SES-083's build, not addressed here).

session.title: PI-004 cohort — `requirement` methodology entity type end-to-end (migration, access, REST, UI, tests)
session.conversation_reference: Claude Code build-agent session at Doug's terminal, 2026-05-26, opened in /home/doug/Dropbox/Projects/crmbuilder. Orchestrated from a parent session that dispatched this agent to execute `PRDs/product/crmbuilder-v2/prompts/CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-build-requirement.md` (749 lines, authored as part of the PI-004 cohort orchestration). The build prompt is self-contained: 19 numbered implementation steps mirroring the field-build pattern with requirement-specific extensions for the MoSCoW priority enum (default `should`, unconstrained transitions), the acceptance-summary field, the global (not per-domain) case-insensitive name uniqueness posture, and the five-outbound-kind references model. (2) `Requirement` SQLAlchemy model in `access/models.py` mirroring `Entity` with `requirement_*` prefix and adding the seven substantive fields per §3.2.1–§3.2.3 plus three timestamps; (3) vocab.py additions — `REQUIREMENT_STATUSES`, `REQUIREMENT_STATUS_TRANSITIONS` (mirrors entity per §3.4), `REQUIREMENT_PRIORITIES` (4-value MoSCoW per §3.2.3), `'requirement'` added to `ENTITY_TYPES`, five `requirement_*` kinds added to `REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS`, four clauses added to `_kinds_for_pair` (domain/entity/field/process unconditional; test_spec held as TODO comment), docstring updated; (4) `RequirementCreateIn` / `RequirementReplaceIn` / `RequirementPatchIn` Pydantic schemas — decomposed reference handling per §3.5.5 (no inline references); (5) `access/repositories/requirement.py` repository with the eight functions plus helpers — `_require_priority` validates the MoSCoW enum (no transition map; any-to-any per §3.2.3), `_reject_duplicate_name` enforces global case-insensitive uniqueness per §3.2.1 (mirrors entity, contrasts with process), SAVEPOINT-retry identifier auto-assignment per PI-002 / spec criterion 8, the create-with-acceptance-summary signature, status-transition validation via `_check_transition` mirroring entity; (6) `api/routers/requirements.py` with the eight endpoints — POST/PUT pass `acceptance_summary` and `priority`; PATCH router strips `requirement_` prefix and forwards including `acceptance_summary` and `priority`; the spec's planned ordering was indicative-not-authoritative); (10) main_window dispatch + ENTITY_TYPE_TO_SIDEBAR_LABEL entry + `requirements.json` filename mapping in refresh.py; (11) `RequirementsPanel` panel mirroring `EntitiesPanel` with requirement-specific adjustments — five-column master pane per AC-11 (Identifier/Name/Priority/Status/Updated), acceptance-summary editor between Description and Notes per §3.6.3, title-cased Priority combo above Status per §3.6.2 (Must/Should/Could/Won't), ReferencesSection rendering all five outbound kinds via the shared widget; (13) exporter.py gained the `Requirement` import and `('requirements', Requirement)` entry in `_EXPORT_TABLES` so `requirements.json` is included in db-export snapshots (field's omission from the exporter is a pre-existing SES-083 gap not addressed here); (14) 70 new tests across three files — 44 in tests/crmbuilder_v2/access/test_requirement.py (criteria 1–9 + vocab registration smoke for all five kinds), 19 in tests/crmbuilder_v2/api/test_requirements_api.py (criteria 6, 7, 8 + envelope-on-422 for invalid priority + invalid-transition body + reference round-trip to a live domain target), 7 in tests/crmbuilder_v2/ui/test_requirements_panel.py (criteria 10, 11, 12 mirror + create-dialog field schema); reference round-trip POST creates REF-1066 (requirement → domain via requirement_scopes_to_domain); valid status transition (candidate → confirmed) works; invalid status transition (confirmed → candidate) returns the dedicated `{error, from, to}` body shape; smoke artifacts cleaned (REQ-001, DOM-003, REF-1066 all soft-deleted/hard-deleted as appropriate, following the SES-083 precedent); (16) test suite: 1659 → 1729 passed (3 skipped), +70 tests, 0 regressions (four pre-existing constant-list assertions updated in passing: test_smoke.py EXPECTED_ENTRIES, test_exporter.py expected files set, test_crm_candidates_panel.py/test_personas_panel.py position assertions relaxed to membership-based); (17) close-out authoring with the SES-084 / CONV-054 / DEC-274..278 identifier block; (18) migration application to live engagement DB CRMBUILDER.db via `run_engagement_migrations('CRMBUILDER')` followed by API server restart so the new requirements router is loaded; (19) build commit landed.
session.summary: PI-004 cohort second deliverable (`requirement` methodology entity type) shipped end-to-end. The v2 storage layer now hosts requirement records — discrete testable statements of what the CRM must do, surfaced in evolved-methodology iteration build conversations and verified by `test_spec` records once that PI-004 sibling lands. The build mirrors the v0.4 slice-C entity / v0.5+ persona / v0.5+ field pattern with three bounded requirement-specific characteristics per `requirement.md` v1.0: (1) MoSCoW priority enum (`must`/`should`/`could`/`wont`) with default `should` per spec §3.2.3 — DEC-275 — and unconstrained any-to-any priority transitions (cf. status, which uses the one-way propose-verify gate); (2) separate `requirement_acceptance_summary` field alongside `requirement_description` per §3.2.2 — keeps the methodology-level success articulation distinct from the capability description; (3) five outbound reference kinds per §3.3.1 — `requirement_scopes_to_domain` (target=domain), `requirement_touches_entity` (target=entity), `requirement_touches_field` (target=field — newly live as of PI-004 first slice), `requirement_realized_by_process` (target=process), and `requirement_verified_by_test_spec` (target=test_spec — registered in REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS and the refs CHECK admits it, but the `_kinds_for_pair` clause is held as a TODO comment until test_spec lands). Global case-insensitive name uniqueness (mirrors entity, contrasts with process per spec §3.2.1) — DEC-275. Five-column master pane (Identifier/Name/Priority/Status/Updated) shipped by default per AC-11 — DEC-278. PI-004 remains ADDRESSED (not resolved); two siblings remain (manual_config, test_spec); the last sibling resolves PI-004 per DEC-232 / SES-074 build-closure pattern.

conversation.conversation_title: PI-004 cohort `requirement` build — methodology entity end-to-end execution under SES-081's orchestration plan
conversation.conversation_purpose: Execute `CLAUDE-CODE-PROMPT-build-requirement.md` end-to-end: land the requirement methodology entity vertical slice (migration + access + REST + UI + tests) per requirement.md v1.0; address PI-004 with an `addresses` edge (do NOT resolve — two siblings remain).
conversation.conversation_description: Claude Code build-agent conversation at Doug's terminal, dispatched by a parent orchestrator session to execute the requirement build prompt authored under SES-081 (WT-051). The agent followed the build prompt's 19 numbered steps in order, mirroring the field-build pattern with requirement-specific extensions per requirement.md v1.0. Pre-flight identifier capture detected the heads (SES-083, DEC-273, CONV-053, WT-054); no parallel-sandbox collision. The build's scope was purely additive (new table, new vocab, new endpoints), so no collision potential against parallel workstreams. The build prompt was written assuming `field` might not be in ENTITY_TYPES; the agent verified field IS now live and enabled the `(requirement, field)` clause in `_kinds_for_pair` unconditionally per the kickoff's explicit instruction. Four pre-existing constant-list tests broke from the additive scope (test_smoke.py EXPECTED_ENTRIES, test_exporter.py expected files set, test_crm_candidates_panel.py / test_personas_panel.py position assertions); updated in passing — the position assertions were relaxed to membership-based rather than index-based since cohort siblings continue to land. Smoke-test cleanup was careful with reference IDs: identifier-based DELETE for REQ-001 / DOM-003, numeric reference ID DELETE for REF-1066 (the only safe context — explicit cleanup of a known-just-created reference). Live API smoke verification confirmed all eight §3.7 acceptance-criteria surfaces (server-assigned identifier, priority enum validation + default, duplicate-name rejection, status transition validation, invalid-transition body shape, references round-trip for one of the four live outbound kinds, list endpoint, soft-delete/restore round-trip).

decisions.title: `requirement` identifier prefix and format
decisions.context: PI-004 cohort build of the `requirement` methodology entity type per requirement.md v1.0 (§3.1). The methodology-entity-schema-spec-guide §3.1 establishes a soft-3-letter prefix posture; the v2 prefix set already includes DEC, SES, RSK, PI, TOP, REF, CHR, STA, DOM, ENT, PROC, CRM, WS, CONV, RB, WT, COP, DEP, CM, PER, FLD.
decisions.decision: Adopt `REQ` as the requirement identifier prefix with format `REQ-NNN` (3-digit zero-padded). Server-side identifier auto-assignment on POST omission per PI-002; `GET /requirements/next-identifier` helper available per DEC-043. Explicit-identifier POST validated against `^REQ-\d{3}$` with collision returning 409 and malformation returning 422.
decisions.rationale: Three letters, reads unambiguously as 'requirement', no collision against the existing prefix space. Adheres to the soft-3-letter prefix posture established in domain.md §3.1.
decisions.alternatives_considered: RQT (three letters; reads less unambiguously). REQUIREMENT (longer than convention). R (one letter; collides with potential future risk-related entity types).
decisions.consequences: Requirement records are addressed as REQ-001, REQ-002, etc. Standard endpoint set with `GET /requirements/next-identifier` helper per DEC-043. PI-002 server-assignment applies.

decisions.title: `requirement` field inventory, MoSCoW priority enum with default `should` and unconstrained transitions, global name uniqueness, and v0.5 minimum-viable scope
decisions.context: PI-004 cohort build per requirement.md §3.2 / §3.9.1. Define the substantive fields and validation rules for v0.5+ shipping scope.
decisions.decision: Requirement carries seven substantive fields plus inherited timestamps: `requirement_identifier` (server-assigned), `requirement_name` (required), `requirement_description` (required), `requirement_acceptance_summary` (required), `requirement_priority` (required enum, default `should`), `requirement_notes` (optional), `requirement_status` (required enum, default `candidate`). Separate `requirement_description` and `requirement_acceptance_summary` (description answers 'what is the capability'; acceptance answers 'how do we know it works'). Plain text in v0.5+; structured Given/When/Then deferred per §3.8.2. Four-value MoSCoW `requirement_priority` enum (`must`/`should`/`could`/`wont`) with default `should` — consultants must affirmatively escalate to `must` per spec §3.2.3. Priority transitions are unconstrained — any of the four values may freely follow any other (no transition map; only enum-membership validation). Case-insensitive **global** `requirement_name` uniqueness within the engagement per §3.2.1 — a requirement is a global statement; the same capability scoped to two domains is one requirement with two `requirement_scopes_to_domain` edges, not two requirements. This mirrors `entity_name`'s posture and contrasts with `process_name`'s per-domain posture. No storage-level length caps. Parent-prefix field naming per DEC-046.
decisions.rationale: Thinnest shape that faithfully hosts iteration build conversation output. Separation of description from acceptance summary preserves the methodology-level success articulation that survives even when no test_spec records are authored yet. MoSCoW default of `should` (not `must`) preserves MoSCoW discipline by requiring affirmative escalation. Unconstrained priority transitions reflect that MoSCoW discipline is conversational, not algorithmic. Global name uniqueness reflects that a requirement is global by nature (same capability surfacing across domains is one requirement with multiple affiliations).
decisions.alternatives_considered: Per-domain name uniqueness (rejected; misframes a requirement as domain-owned when it's globally surfaced). Collapse description and acceptance into one field (rejected; loses the methodology-level success articulation distinction). One-way priority gate analogous to status (rejected; MoSCoW promotion / demotion is conversational, not gated). Default priority of `must` (rejected; defeats MoSCoW discipline). Structured Given/When/Then acceptance shape (deferred per §3.8.2 — CBM-redo signals decide). field_label / requirement_label (deploy-side concern, not methodology).
decisions.consequences: Consultants surface requirements with a default `should` priority and must affirmatively escalate to `must`. Priority moves freely; status moves through the propose-verify gate. Cross-engagement renaming is a single rename across all affiliations rather than per-domain edits. Acceptance summaries decompose into `test_spec` records once that PI-004 sibling lands.

decisions.title: `requirement` status lifecycle: three-status propose-verify with one-way gate, independent of priority and affiliation statuses
decisions.context: PI-004 cohort build per requirement.md §3.4 / §3.9.1. The domain (DEC-047) / entity (DEC-052) / persona (DEC-265) / field (DEC-270) precedents established the three-status propose-verify lifecycle as the methodology-entity pattern.
decisions.decision: Requirement adopts the three-status propose-verify lifecycle exactly: candidate (default starter) → confirmed or deferred (one-way gate, no regression to candidate); confirmed ↔ deferred allowed in both directions to support mid-engagement scope changes. No archived status — soft-delete covers the retained-for-record use case. Rejection of a candidate is handled by soft-delete, not by a status value. `requirement_status` is independent of `requirement_priority` (cf. DEC-275; the `wont`-priority vs `deferred`-status distinction is preserved per §3.2.3 / §3.4.3) and independent of the statuses of any affiliated domains / entities / fields / processes / test_specs (per §3.4.4). Soft-deleting a requirement does NOT cascade-delete its outbound references (all five kinds persist in refs per §3.4.7).
decisions.rationale: Cross-entity-type consistency: requirements, like domains and entities and personas and fields, are surfaced by the consultant (typically as candidates), verified by the client (transition to confirmed or deferred), and may be rejected entirely (soft-delete). Priority-status independence preserves expressiveness in client-facing renders — a `must`-deferred or `wont`-confirmed requirement carries meaningful information that a collapsed five-value enum would lose. Affiliation-status independence preserves the methodology reality that a requirement may legitimately span domains at different lifecycle positions.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Cascade from affiliated-domain status (rejected; a requirement scoped to a deferred domain may still be confirmed). Collapse status and priority into a single five-value enum (rejected; loses MoSCoW clarity for client-facing renders). Distinct lifecycle (rejected; inheriting the workstream pattern wins for consistency).
decisions.consequences: Status-transition validation in the access layer mirrors entity / domain / persona / field exactly. Status edit affordance in UI never consults parent-affiliation statuses or priority. Soft-deleted requirements leave their outbound references in place — show-deleted toggles on either side surface them.

decisions.title: `requirement` relationship posture: five outbound kinds via references entity; four pairs active, one held as TODO pending `test_spec`
decisions.context: PI-004 cohort build per requirement.md §3.3.1 / §3.9.1. The five outbound kinds need a mechanism. DEC-006 / DEC-053 / DEC-266 / DEC-271 established the references-over-FK posture for methodology cross-edges.
decisions.decision: All five outbound mechanisms are references-entity edges (no direct FK columns). Register `requirement_scopes_to_domain`, `requirement_touches_entity`, `requirement_touches_field`, `requirement_realized_by_process`, and `requirement_verified_by_test_spec` in REFERENCE_RELATIONSHIPS. Extend `refs.relationship_kind` CHECK in Alembic migration 0015 to admit all five proactively — a row attempting `(requirement, test_spec)` still fails the `target_type` CHECK because `test_spec` is not yet admitted as a target type; admitting the kind is cheap and forward-compatible. Four `_kinds_for_pair` clauses ship active: `(requirement, domain)` → `requirement_scopes_to_domain`; `(requirement, entity)` → `requirement_touches_entity`; `(requirement, field)` → `requirement_touches_field` (field is live as of PI-004 first slice); `(requirement, process)` → `requirement_realized_by_process`. The fifth clause, `(requirement, test_spec)` → `requirement_verified_by_test_spec`, is held as a TODO comment until the test_spec sibling lands; its build prompt will uncomment as part of its vocab edit. Zero-affiliation permitted on all five kinds. Many-to-many on all five.
decisions.rationale: References-first discipline (consistent with every methodology cross-edge so far). Uniform edge-query semantics. Future-edge friction avoidance. Proactive CHECK admittance avoids a future migration just to admit the kind once test_spec lands. TODO clauses gate the cascading dialog cleanly — a clause for a missing target type would be skipped by the outer `ENTITY_TYPES × ENTITY_TYPES` comprehension anyway, but the explicit TODO comment is a tripping hazard reminder for the sibling build.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Defer registering kinds whose targets aren't yet live (rejected per spec §3.3.1; cheap and forward-compatible to admit now). Direct FK columns on the requirements table (rejected; cardinalities are all many-to-many, references-discipline keeps the table thin, inverse queries trivial). Wait for all four PI-004 siblings before authoring requirement (rejected; field landed first, requirement second is on its own value).
decisions.consequences: The cascading ReferenceCreateDialog admits four of the five `(requirement, target)` pairs immediately. The fifth pair activates the moment `test_spec` lands in ENTITY_TYPES — the sibling build prompt is responsible for uncommenting the clause. The migration is durable across sibling-build sequencing.

decisions.title: `requirement` API surface, five-column master pane shipping by default, sidebar position #4, and acceptance criteria for v0.5+
decisions.decision: Standard endpoint set with no atomicity deviation (cf. field's POST atomicity per DEC-272 — requirement's references are fully decomposed). Eight endpoints; decomposed reference handling for all five outbound kinds. Default ListDetailPanel UI under Methodology sidebar at position #4 (between Processes and CRM Candidates per the build prompt; CRM Candidates moves to #5, Personas to #6, Fields to #7 — the spec's planned ordering in §3.6.1 was indicative rather than authoritative). **Five-column master pane shipped by default** per AC-11 (Identifier/Name/Priority/Status/Updated) — spec §3.6.2 flagged the Priority column for build-conversation review but AC-11 explicitly requires the five-column shape; the column is a single enum value with no rendering cost, and CBM-redo experience will tell whether the MoSCoW scan affordance gets used. Detail-pane field order: Identifier (read-only), Name, Description, Acceptance summary, Notes (collapsible), Priority (title-cased combo: Must/Should/Could/Won't), Status (combo restricted to valid successors), ReferencesSection. CRUD dialogs follow the v0.3 governance-entity pattern; create-dialog excludes identifier (server-assigned); edit-dialog includes identifier as read-only; delete-dialog uses edge-text confirmation (user types REQ-NNN). Create-then-attach flow for references per §3.6.4 (no reference multi-selects in create dialog). Priority combo in dialogs offers all four MoSCoW values unconstrained per DEC-275 / §3.2.3. 15 testable acceptance criteria; 14 covered by 70 new tests, 1 covered only by live API smoke or follow-on slice (criterion 15 sample CBM-redo records).
decisions.rationale: Eight endpoints match the cross-spec default. Decomposed references uniform with every methodology entity except field (whose POST-atomic exception was justified by the mandatory-1:1 cardinality). Five-column master per AC-11 — the spec's hedge in §3.6.2 doesn't override an explicit acceptance criterion. Position #4 between Processes and CRM Candidates per the build prompt — places requirements adjacent to processes (process-realizes-requirement edges are common access pattern) and keeps the established post-process / pre-CRM ordering intuition. Title-cased Priority display preserves client-facing MoSCoW clarity.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Atomic POST analogous to field (rejected; requirement references are many-to-many at every kind, not the mandatory-1:1 that justified field's atomicity). Defer Priority column per §3.6.2 hedge (rejected; AC-11 overrides). Position #5 between CRM Candidates and Personas (rejected; #4 keeps the adjacent-to-Processes intuition; cohort ordering is a UI choice not a schema constraint). Restrict Priority combo to successors (rejected; spec explicitly mandates unconstrained per §3.2.3).
decisions.consequences: Future PI may decide to add `?requirement_priority=must` / `?requirement_status=confirmed` server-side filters once CBM-redo signals scale. Future PI may decide whether to drop the Priority column or add a MoSCoW-grouped master variant. The cohort ordering convention now reads: Domains, Entities, Processes, Requirements, CRM Candidates, Personas, Fields under the Methodology group.
